key_insight: A mixed Petrov–Galerkin Cosserat rod FEM with linear interpolation and specific quadrature can be rewritten as a two-node discrete rod model with compliant coupling, while retaining robustness and no-locking behavior.
engineering_relevance: This gives soft robotics and slender-structure simulation a physics-based rod model that is simpler to couple locally than dense reduced-order formulations, yet remains accurate and stable.
method: Represent each rod segment as a pair of rigid nodes linked by compliant force and moment interactions.
Compute discrete dilatation, shear, torsion, and curvature from relative node kinematics.
Use compliance-form constitutive laws with independent stress DOFs; midpoint integration for internal work and trapezoidal rules for external/inertial terms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports three numerical examples—piecewise-varying cross sections, tendon-driven actuation at different discretizations, and coupled longitudinal-torsional dynamics—showing accuracy, robustness, and convergence.
watch_item: The formulation depends on a specific mixed FEM discretization and quadrature choice, so implementation consistency is important to preserve the no-locking and equivalence properties.
paper_title: A Mixed Discrete Cosserat Rod Formulation
